title: Verify Chute Unavailable State When A Tote Is Full
summary: Use this source-based reference check to confirm the documented relationship between a tote reaching the Full state and the chute being marked unavailable so packages do not continue coming down during an exchange.
when_to_use: Use when interpreting or verifying the documented system state for tote exchange conditions, specifically to confirm that a full tote corresponds to a chute-unavailable state intended to prevent additional packages from coming down during an exchange.
not_for: Do not use as a full tote exchange workflow.
Do not use to determine the mechanism, interface, or corrective steps for how Chat is informed, because the source does not provide them.
Do not use as a troubleshooting or repair procedure beyond comparing observed behavior to the documented relationship.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Interpret and verify the documented relationship between tote Full state and chute unavailable state using the source-provided wording.

safety_notes: This candidate is a procedural interpretation/reference and not a physical exchange procedure.
Use only the source-provided wording for the full-tote and chute-unavailable relationship.
Do not invent undocumented controls, interfaces, or corrective actions.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the documented tote and chute state information

access_required: Access to the documented tote and chute state information
system_preconditions: A tote state can be identified against the source-defined thresholds.
The user is reviewing a condition where tote fullness and chute availability are being interpreted.

visual_references.description: Training slide showing tote fullness thresholds and the statement that when a tote is full, Chat is told the chute is unavailable so no packages come down during an exchange.

steps.title: Identify the tote Full state
steps.purpose: Establish that the condition being interpreted is the source-defined Full tote state.
steps.instruction: Identify that the tote has reached the source-defined "Full" state rather than only "Pretty full."
steps.expected_result: The tote condition is recognized as Full according to the source thresholds.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote is identified as being in the Full state, which the source defines as no more product being diverted to that location until an exchange is done.
steps.failure_condition: The tote state cannot be confirmed as Full, or it is only interpreted as Pretty full.

actions.label: Confirm Full threshold
actions.exact_action: Identify that the tote has reached the source-defined "Full" state.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide text describing the two thresholds: "Pretty full" and "Full," and the wording that Full means no more product can be diverted until an exchange is done.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the tote cannot be confirmed as being in the Full state using the source-supported thresholds.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Two thresholds exist: "Pretty full" — not full, but getting close enough to start considering an exchange; "Full" — no more product can be diverted to this location until an exchange is done.

steps.title: Check the documented chute unavailable behavior
steps.purpose: Confirm the source-stated relationship between a full tote and the chute being marked unavailable.
steps.instruction: Check the documented system behavior that when a tote is full, Chat is told that the chute is unavailable.
steps.expected_result: The documented relationship between a full tote and chute unavailable is confirmed from the source.
steps.healthy_condition: The source wording clearly ties the Full tote condition to Chat being told the chute is unavailable.
steps.failure_condition: The documented relationship cannot be confirmed from the source material being reviewed.

actions.label: Review chute unavailable statement
actions.exact_action: Check the documented system behavior that when a tote is full, Chat is told that the chute is unavailable.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The statement on the slide that when a tote is full, the system tells Chat that the chute is unavailable.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed system behavior does not align with the documented full-tote and chute-unavailable relationship.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a tote is full, we tell Chat that the chute is unavailable so that no packages come down while doing an exchange.

steps.title: Verify the intended meaning of chute unavailable
steps.purpose: Interpret the source-provided purpose of the chute unavailable state during exchange.
steps.instruction: Verify that the expected meaning of chute unavailable is that packages should not continue coming down while doing an exchange.
steps.expected_result: The chute unavailable state is understood as preventing packages from continuing to come down during the exchange.
steps.healthy_condition: The chute unavailable state is interpreted consistently with the source wording: no packages come down while doing an exchange.
steps.failure_condition: The chute unavailable state is interpreted differently than the source wording, or observed behavior suggests packages continue coming down during exchange.

actions.label: Interpret chute unavailable purpose
actions.exact_action: Verify that the expected meaning of chute unavailable is that packages should not continue coming down while doing an exchange.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The wording that the chute is unavailable so that no packages come down while doing an exchange.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if observed behavior does not align with the documented intent that no packages should come down during an exchange.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a tote is full, we tell Chat that the chute is unavailable so that no packages come down while doing an exchange.

steps.title: Record the source-grounded relationship
steps.purpose: Document the interpretation using only the wording supported by the source.
steps.instruction: Record that the chute-unavailable state is tied to the full tote condition, using only the source-provided wording.
steps.expected_result: A source-grounded note is captured that a full tote causes the chute to be unavailable so packages do not continue coming down during an exchange.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded note stays within the source wording and does not add undocumented mechanism or corrective detail.
steps.failure_condition: The recorded note adds unsupported explanation, mechanism, interface detail, or corrective steps not present in the source.

actions.label: Document source wording
actions.exact_action: Record that the chute-unavailable state is tied to the full tote condition, using only the source-provided wording.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The slide section showing the statement that a full tote makes the chute unavailable during exchange.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if documenting the condition would require adding unsupported mechanism, interface, or corrective steps.
Escalate if the observed system behavior does not align with the documented full-tote and chute-unavailable relationship.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a tote is full, we tell Chat that the chute is unavailable so that no packages come down while doing an exchange.

success_criteria: The user confirms from the source that a tote in the Full state is associated with the chute being marked unavailable.
The user confirms from the source that the purpose of chute unavailable is to prevent packages from coming down during an exchange.
The interpretation is documented using only source-supported wording.
healthy_conditions: The tote is identified as Full according to the source-defined thresholds.
The chute unavailable state is understood as the expected condition when the tote is full.
No packages should continue coming down while doing an exchange, per the source statement.
failure_conditions: The tote cannot be confirmed as Full using the source-supported thresholds.
Observed behavior does not align with the documented relationship between full tote and chute unavailable.
The interpretation requires unsupported assumptions about how Chat is informed or how the system should be corrected.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed system behavior does not align with the documented full-tote and chute-unavailable relationship.
Escalate if additional mechanism, interface, or corrective detail is needed, because the source does not provide it.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Slide section showing tote thresholds and the statement that when a tote is full, Chat is told the chute is unavailable so no packages come down during an exchange.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: When a tote is full, we tell Chat that the chute is unavailable so that no packages come down while doing an exchange.

missing_details: The source does not provide the mechanism or interface by which Chat is informed that the chute is unavailable.
The source does not provide corrective actions if observed behavior does not match the documented relationship.
The source does not provide a full tote exchange workflow in this candidate.
The source does not provide a time estimate for performing this verification.
